prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of benzaldehyde was computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', PySCF RKS backend) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). The N±1 states used charge ±1 with multiplicity 2 for finite-difference Fukui index construction. The input geometry was used as-is from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization at DFT level). SCF converged for all three states (neutral, cation, anion); Fukui charge conservation checks passed (sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f- ) = 1.0000). Integrity: trustworthy = true. No warnings reported. Headline result: most electrophilic site is Atom 3 (C, the carbonyl carbon) with f+ = 0.39938966; most nucleophilic site is Atom 1 (O, the carbonyl oxygen) with f- = 0.23839906.
